_article: XXXIX.7 — 'Terrain is the LAST concern, because terrain is merely the block-level visualization of a world whose semantic state (traversable, steep, river, forest, spirit-rich) already exists in the compiled state.' User directive: 'I'd do terrain almost last.'
_description: Schema for the COMPILED Biome Lookup — the point-to-biome mapping the Minecraft adapter queries AT RENDER TIME (the LAST step). Derived from country_lookup (country -> biome_rule) plus local semantic modifiers (spirit-rich overlay, river proximity, forest density). The simulation does NOT query this for gameplay; it queries the semantic fields directly. This lookup exists solely so the chunk renderer knows how to paint blocks.

biome_rules.country_id: string — the country whose interior uses this biome

semantic_modifiers.spirit_rich_overlay: string|null — biome variant where spirit_graph reports high qi density
semantic_modifiers.river_proximity_overlay: string|null — biome variant within N blocks of a river (from hydrology_graph)
semantic_modifiers.forest_density_overlay: string|null — biome variant in high-density Forest entities
semantic_modifiers.elevation_band_overlay: string|null — biome variant at high elevation (from MountainRange facts)

biome_rules.lod_fidelity_profile: string — reference to lod_regions entry for this biome's render fidelity at distance

queries_supported: biome_at(x, z) -> biome_id — derived from country_at(x,z) -> biome_rule + local semantic modifiers
block_palette_at(x, z) -> {surface, subsurface, stone, fluid} — what the chunk renderer should paint

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — Country.biome_rule + semantic modifier overlays from spirit_graph/hydrology_graph/Forest entities
_runtime_consumers: ChunkRenderer (Minecraft adapter) — THE ONLY consumer. The simulation never queries this; it queries semantic state directly.
_note: This is the boundary between the authored/compiled world and Minecraft. Everything above this layer is simulation; everything at and below is rendering. Article XXXIX.7: build the living state first, render it last.
